message:
feat(core,btrfs): implement btrfs punch-hole fallocate and harden compressed extent handling

ffs-core: Implement FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE|FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE for btrfs
write path — zero-fills the requested byte range while preserving file
size and unaffected data. Add ram_bytes overflow guard (try_from instead
of bare cast). Reformat btrfs_decompress error map closures to satisfy
clippy/fmt. Expand btrfs compressed Regular extent reading with proper
offset calculations into decompressed data. Additional hardening for
extent lookup edge cases.

ffs-btrfs: Add punch_hole_supported flag to BtrfsExtentData parsing
for feature detection.

ffs-alloc: Add batch allocation benchmark setup. Expand allocator
coverage.

ffs-extent: Minor extent handling improvement.

ffs-ondisk: Ext4 test adjustments for updated geometry.

FEATURE_PARITY.md / README.md: Update btrfs fallocate punch-hole from
"unsupported" to "supported (experimental)" with zero-fill semantics.
Rename test from btrfs_write_fallocate_punch_hole_rejected to
btrfs_write_fallocate_punch_hole_zeroes_data. Update E2E scenario name.

scripts/e2e: Update btrfs smoke and hardening gate E2E scripts to
exercise the new punch-hole success path instead of rejection path.

Update beads issue tracker with punch-hole and related status changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"bd-4ogh","title":"Implement ext4/btrfs parity features: decompression, inline data, bigalloc, ZERO_RANGE","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:28:47.537528441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.634096851Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.633525312Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 new parity features:
(1) btrfs transparent decompression: ZLIB (flate2) + ZSTD in read path for inline and regular extents. Added ram_bytes field to BtrfsExtentData enum. LZO deferred (custom framing).
(2) ext4 inline data read: reads from inode extent_bytes + xattr_ibody when EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL is set. Removed INLINE_DATA from REJECTED_V1 incompat set.
(3) ext4 bigalloc infrastructure: added cluster_ratio to FsGeometry (cluster_size/block_size for BIGALLOC, else 1).
(4) ext4 ZERO_RANGE fallocate: writes zero blocks to existing allocated extents in range. Mode 0x10 now accepted.
All tests pass (471 ffs-core, 360 ffs-ondisk, 142 ffs-alloc, 117 ffs-btrfs, 114 ffs-extent).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ext4","parity"]}

 {"id":"bd-6tme.2","title":"Adoption wedge: deterministic MVCC snapshot isolation demo","description":"# Adoption wedge: deterministic MVCC snapshot isolation demo

## GOAL
Single-command demo showing MVCC snapshot isolation on a filesystem: two concurrent readers see consistent snapshots while a writer modifies files. The adoption wedge for the MVCC story.

## DELIVERABLES
1. `cargo run --example mvcc_demo` -- creates image, starts 2 read transactions + 1 write transaction, shows isolation
2. Output shows: reader A sees version 1, writer commits version 2, reader A still sees version 1, reader B (started after commit) sees version 2
3. No FUSE mount needed -- operates on raw image via API
4. Deterministic, < 3 seconds

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: demo exits 0 with expected output pattern
2. Integration: snapshot isolation invariant verified

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Transaction start (info): txn_id, snapshot_seq
- Read (debug): txn_id, block, version_seen
- Write commit (info): txn_id, commit_seq, blocks_written
- Isolation check (info): reader_txn, expected_version, actual_version, isolated=true/false

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Single command, < 3 seconds
2. Demonstrates snapshot isolation visually
3. Deterministic
4.  {"id":"bd-6tme.3","title":"E2E test: self-healing demo runs successfully and produces correct output","description":"# E2E test: self-healing demo runs successfully and produces correct output

## GOAL
E2E test for the Killer Demo epic (bd-6tme). Runs the demo script end-to-end and verifies the output format, repair correctness, and timing.

## TEST SCENARIO
1. Run `cargo run --bin ffs-demo -- self-healing` (or equivalent)
2. Verify exit code 0
3. Parse output: verify corruption count, repair count, verification pass
4. Verify: corrupted blocks == repaired blocks (zero data loss)
5. Verify: evidence ledger present and parseable
6. Verify: total time < 30 seconds

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Demo runs without error
2. All corrupted blocks repaired
3. Output matches expected format
4. Timing within bounds
5.  {"id":"bd-7tn4","title":"PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)","description":"# PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)

## GOAL
Implement Proofs of Retrievability (PoR) for cryptographic verification that stored data is intact and retrievable, without reading the entire dataset.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 11.11: PoR (Juels-Kaliski 2007, Shacham-Waters 2008) allows a verifier to challenge a storage system with random queries. The storage system responds with proofs that specific blocks exist and are correct. If the proofs verify, the entire dataset is retrievable with high probability.

## DESIGN
- Challenge: verifier sends random block indices + random coefficients
- Response: storage computes linear combination of challenged blocks weighted by coefficients
- Verification: verifier checks response against precomputed authenticators
- Authenticators: computed once at file creation, stored alongside data

## APPLICABILITY
- Future feature: useful for cloud/remote storage verification
- Complementary to RaptorQ: PoR proves data exists, RaptorQ repairs it if it doesn't
- Useful for auditing: periodic PoR challenges prove filesystem integrity without full scan

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] PoR authenticator generation at file creation
- [ ] Challenge-response protocol implemented
- [ ] Verification: false positive rate < 2^-128
- [ ] Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file","acceptance_criteria":"PoR authenticator generation at file creation time. Challenge-response protocol implemented with configurable challenge count. Verification: false positive rate below 2^-128. Authenticators stored alongside data in reserved metadata area. Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file. Benchmark: authenticator generation time per MB. Unit tests: generate authenticators, challenge, verify for small files. Unit tests: corrupted block causes verification failure. Unit tests: uncorrupted file passes all challenges. Property test: random challenge sets always verify on intact data.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:32.840799452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:37:09.585683263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:37:09.585575381Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crypto","durability","por"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:32.828805088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-820","title":"Implement RaptorQ symbol refresh on dirty group flush","description":"# Implement RaptorQ symbol refresh on dirty group flush

## GOAL
Ensure repair symbols are regenerated when block groups are modified, as mandated by spec §0.4: "A block write that never triggers repair symbol refresh for its group is a spec-conformance bug."

## BACKGROUND
Currently repair symbols are generated at image creation. When blocks are modified:
1. Group becomes "stale" (symbols no longer match data)
2. Must regenerate before corruption can be recovered
3. Two modes: lazy (on next scrub) or eager (on fsync)

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Dirty Group Tracking
```rust
pub struct DirtyGroupTracker {
    dirty: BitVec,  // One bit per group
}

impl DirtyGroupTracker {
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self, group: GroupNumber);
    pub fn is_dirty(&self, group: GroupNumber) -> bool;
    pub fn clear(&mut self, group: GroupNumber);
    pub fn dirty_groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = GroupNumber>;
}
```

### 2. Refresh Triggers
- **Lazy**: Background scrub regenerates symbols for dirty groups
- **Eager (fsync)**: Regenerate immediately for committed groups
- **Configurable**: MountConfig.repair_refresh_policy

### 3. Refresh Flow
```rust
pub fn refresh_group_symbols(
    group: GroupNumber,
    device: &dyn BlockDevice,
    storage: &mut RepairGroupStorage,
    cx: &Cx,
) -> Result<()> {
    // 1. Read all blocks in group
    // 2. Regenerate RaptorQ symbols
    // 3. Write to repair storage slots
    // 4. Clear dirty flag
}
```

### 4. Budget Integration
- Refresh respects Cx budget
- Can be interrupted and resumed
- Progress tracked per-group

## TESTS
1. Unit: Dirty tracking set/clear
2. Integration: Write marks group dirty
3. Integration: Scrub refreshes dirty group
4. Integration: fsync refreshes if eager mode
5. E2E: Corruption after refresh -> recovery works

## LOGGING
- Mark dirty (trace): group number, cause
- Refresh start (debug): group, block count
- Refresh complete (info): group, duration, symbol count

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Every modified group eventually refreshed
2. Lazy mode: refreshed within scrub interval
3. Eager mode: refreshed on fsync
4. Recovery works for refreshed groups","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:43.407560046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T14:08:46.778165832Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T14:08:46.778143631Z","close_reason":"Implemented dirty-group refresh APIs (mark/query/flush), aligned refresh logging, and added flush-path + tracker tests in ffs-repair pipeline; validated via rch cargo test/check/clippy and cargo fmt --check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cow","raptorq","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-820","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:20.818428330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-820","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:49.899554131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-820","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

+{"id":"bd-85ja","title":"Implement btrfs transparent decompression (ZLIB + ZSTD)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:00:35.647767488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:00:49.704792242Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:00:49.704020800Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs transparent decompression in the read path:
- Added btrfs_decompress() dispatcher for ZLIB (type 1) and ZSTD (type 3) via flate2 and zstd crates
- LZO (type 2) returns clear UnsupportedFeature error (custom framing format, deferred)
- Updated both inline and regular extent read paths to decompress instead of rejecting
- Added ram_bytes field to BtrfsExtentData enum (Inline and Regular variants) for decompressed size tracking
- Updated parse_extent_data to read ram_bytes from offset 8 of extent item
- Updated to_bytes serialization to write ram_bytes correctly
- Fixed 10 BtrfsExtentData construction sites across ffs-btrfs and ffs-core
- Updated 2 tests from expect-unsupported to expect-corruption (invalid compression data detection)
471 ffs-core tests + 117 ffs-btrfs tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","compression","parity"]}

Context:

- The repo uses rust-toolchain.toml for nightly.

Deliverables:
- Ensure PLAN/README/COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC consistently state the toolchain policy.
- If we claim an MSRV, it must be technically compatible with edition + dependencies.

 {"id":"bd-8fpo","title":"ffs-cli evidence streaming parser fails on non-UTF8 torn lines","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found behavior regression: load_evidence_records used BufRead::lines and could return InvalidData on non-UTF8 corrupted lines, unlike prior tolerant parser behavior.  {"id":"bd-8tr","title":"ext4: Implement inode location math helper (inode -> table block + offset)","description":"Goal: given (inode_number, superblock fields, group desc), compute where the inode bytes live on disk.

Deliverables:
- Pure helper: inode_number -> (group, index_in_group).
- Use group desc inode_table pointer and inode_size to compute:
  - table_start_block
  - inode_byte_offset_from_table_start
  - absolute byte offset on device
- Handle ext4 inode numbering rules (inode numbers start at 1; 0 invalid).

Acceptance:
- Unit tests for boundary inodes: 1, inodes_per_group, inodes_per_group+1, last inode.
- Overflow/invalid inputs produce ParseError/FfsError (no panics).
- Helper is used by higher-level inode read code (ffs-inode later).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:17:15.392537885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:37:56.483745546Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:37:56.483718756Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8tr","depends_on_id":"bd-2w9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.687704093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-qpt5","title":"Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation","description":"# Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation

## GOAL
Accelerate CRC32C and BLAKE3 checksum computation using SIMD instructions.

## BACKGROUND (Extreme Software Optimization)
Checksum computation is on the hot path:
- Every block read/write verifies checksum
- ARC cache hits still verify (trust but verify)
- RaptorQ symbols have checksums

SIMD can provide 4-16x speedup depending on instruction set.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. CRC32C Acceleration
```rust
pub fn crc32c_simd(data: &[u8], seed: u32) -> u32 {
    #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
    {
        if is_x86_feature_detected!("sse4.2") {
            return crc32c_sse42(data, seed);
        }
    }
    #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]
    {
        if is_aarch64_feature_detected!("crc") {
            return crc32c_arm_crc(data, seed);
        }
    }
    crc32c_fallback(data, seed)
}
```

### 2. BLAKE3 (Already SIMD)
- blake3 crate already uses SIMD
- Ensure proper feature flags enabled
- Benchmark to confirm

### 3. Batch Checksum
```rust
/// Compute checksums for multiple blocks in parallel
pub fn batch_checksum(blocks: &[&[u8]], algo: ChecksumAlgo) -> Vec<u32> {
    // Use rayon for multi-block parallelism
    // Use SIMD within each block
}
```

### 4. Feature Detection at Runtime
- Detect CPU features once at startup
- Store in global (or thread-local)
- Dispatch to appropriate implementation

## TESTS
1. Unit: SIMD matches fallback results
2. Unit: All supported CPUs tested (via CI matrix)
3. Benchmark: Before/after comparison
4. Property: Random data, SIMD == fallback

## LOGGING
- Feature detection (info): detected SIMD capabilities
- Batch checksum (trace): block count, duration

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Correctness: SIMD == fallback for all inputs
2. Performance: >2x speedup on supported CPUs
3. Graceful fallback on unsupported CPUs
4. No unsafe code (use safe SIMD wrappers)

## NOTES
Use existing crates:
- crc32c crate has SIMD support
- blake3 crate is already optimized
Main work is ensuring proper feature flags and benchmarking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:14.096946312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163955333Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163936237Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","perf","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.533583752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.930946133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B
Budgeted mode: controller_tick_ms=1000, policy_timeout_ms=200
Fallback trigger: static config profile

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-raks","title":"Fix missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests","description":"## Purpose

Restore workspace compilation by updating stale `FsGeometry` initializers in `ffs-inode` to include the required `desc_size` and `feature_ro_compat` fields.

## Scope

1. Reproduce the current `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` failure via `rch`.
2. Patch the failing `FsGeometry` initializers in `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs`.
3. Re-run targeted `rch` checks for `ffs-inode` and, if clean, the workspace check.

## Acceptance criteria
- `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs` compiles with current `FsGeometry` definition
- Targeted `rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-inode --all-targets` passes
- Workspace check moves past this failure or reports the next unrelated blocker
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T03:40:47.448066803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:09:05.994009750Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T20:09:05.993264249Z","close_reason":"Patched missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests; targeted rch cargo check -p ffs-inode --all-targets passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","inode","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-rale","title":"Improve FfsError::Io errno mapping with ErrorKind fallback","description":"When std::io::Error has no raw_os_error() (e.g. created via Error::new()), to_errno() falls back to EIO. Improve by mapping io::ErrorKind to appropriate errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, etc.) before falling back to EIO. This gives FUSE callers more specific error codes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T00:49:02.204994325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073573327Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073546988Z","close_reason":"Improved to_errno() Io variant: now maps ErrorKind to specific errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, AlreadyExists→EEXIST, WouldBlock→EAGAIN, InvalidInput/InvalidData→EINVAL, TimedOut→ETIMEDOUT, Interrupted→EINTR) when raw_os_error() is None, instead of always falling back to EIO. Added test io_error_kind_mapping_without_raw_errno covering all mapped kinds. Also fixed two pre-existing clippy pedantic lints. All 20 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
